data.title: Singapore's Economic Growth Slowed in First Quarter
data.description:
(00:00 GMT) *Singapore 1Q GDP -0.3% Adj Q/Q; Poll Tipped -0.75%
(00:00 GMT) *Singapore 1Q GDP +4.6% on Year; Poll Tipped +5.7%
(00:00 GMT) *Singapore 1Q Manufacturing Output +5.0% on Year
(00:00 GMT) *Singapore 1Q Construction Output +9.0% on Year
(00:00 GMT) *Singapore 1Q Services Output +4.7% on Year
(00:02 GMT) Singapore's Economic Growth Slowed in First Quarter

By Amanda Lee

SINGAPORE--Singapore's economy lost steam in the first quarter, missing estimates and showing early signs of strain as the Middle East conflict begins to weigh on trade and manufacturing.

Gross domestic product grew 4.6% in the January-to-March period from a year earlier, according to advance estimates released Tuesday.

That compares with the revised 5.7% expansion seen in the fourth quarter and the 5.7% forecast in a poll of economists by The Wall Street Journal.

On a quarter-on-quarter seasonally-adjusted basis, the economy contracted 0.3% in the first quarter. That compared with a revised 1.3% expansion in the fourth quarter and the 0.75% contraction estimated in the WSJ poll.

"While GDP growth remained resilient in the first quarter of 2026, the US-Israel-Iran conflict that began in end-February may weigh on economic activity in the coming quarters," the Ministry of Trade and Industry said.

Write to Amanda Lee at amanda.lee@wsj.com

data.title: Key facts: XRP OI -96%; Bearish signals; 9yr Triangle; $19.3M Inflows
data.description:
• XRP perpetual futures open interest plunged about 96% over six months, from ~7 billion to ~1.5 billion XRP by April 13, 2026; earlier fell to ~2 billion after Oct 2025 crash.1
  • XRPUSD: MACD stays bearish below zero; RSI ~32–33 suggests fading sell pressure but no reversal. Price trades near $1.10–$1.12 support; breakdown could target about $0.95.2
  • XRP formed a nine‑year ascending triangle with support near $0.75–$0.80. Analyst said a breakout could trigger a rally over 500%; current structure points to $0.75–$0.80 as dip entry.3
  • XRP (XRPUSD) funds drew $19.3 million in inflows last week, per CoinShares, marking weekly capital moving into XRP-focused investment products tracked by the report.4

data.title: Key facts: Heavy BTC Accumulation, Bithumb Glitch; Market Signals
data.description:
• One firm bought ~46,233 BTC in March 2026—about three times estimated miner issuance (~16,200 BTC)—signaling heavy accumulation well above new supply as it expands BTC holdings.1
  • BlackRock’s IBIT bought about $600M of BTC last week, lifting its holdings to just under 800,000 BTC (~$56B), roughly 8,030 BTC more than MicroStrategy’s 780,897 BTC.2
  • Bithumb glitch credited ~620,000 BTC to accounts during a promo, triggering mass sell-offs, a local BTCUSD flash crash and forced liquidations; firm blamed internal backlog and weak controls3
  • Bitcoin (BTCUSD) funds drew $872M in inflows last week, lifting year-to-date bitcoin fund inflows to about $2B; bitcoin was the main recipient of last week’s crypto product flows.4
  • Bought 13,927 BTC (~$1B) Apr 6–12 at $71,902 avg, raising holdings to 780,897 BTC. Purchase funded by sale of preferred equity that raised about $1B.5
  • Bank of Korea proposes trading circuit breakers and mandatory on‑chain asset verification plus human‑error payment controls to strengthen Bitcoin (BTCUSD) trading and custody on exchanges6
  • End‑March Bitcoin futures open interest fell to about $56.5B from October 2025 peak, signaling lower leverage; options implied volatility spiked near 45% then eased, matching range‑bound trade.7
  • BTCUSD surged as futures open interest spiked, driven mainly by leveraged futures. Open interest climbed during the rally, then fell as price momentum eased, signaling reduced leverage.8
  • BTCUSD spot ETF AUM steady near $55–$60B in 2026 with little net inflow. Higher U.S. Treasury yields cited; liquidity tailwinds for Bitcoin have weakened, curbing sustained upward momentum.9
  • Analyst Doctor Profit sees Bitcoin likely falling toward $50,000, with a probable rebound to about $76,000 and a smaller chance of reaching $79,000–$84,000 before rejection.10

data.title: Key facts: ETH flows, staking, hack, tech sell signals, SEC ask
data.description:
• ETHUSD saw $196.5M inflows last week; YTD net outflows stand at $130M. Weekly inflows drove crypto product activity; traders note recent capital return despite negative YTD flows.1
  • About 29,900 ETH (~$65.3M) was staked and 32,800+ ETH (~$70M) left exchanges, cutting roughly 62,700 ETH from liquid ETH supply available to traders.2
  • Ethereum-based Hyperbridge flaw let attacker reuse a proof, seize admin on bridged DOT contract, mint ~1B DOT and withdraw ~108.2 ETH. Hyperbridge paused; only DOT bridged via Hyperbridge affected.3
  • ETHUSD momentum easing: RSI ~55, MACD flat. Immediate resistance intact. Support $2,180–$2,200; downside risk near $2,050–$2,000 if support breaks.4
  • ETHUSD 3-day chart shows a death cross: 50-day MA crossed below 200-day MA. Historical patterns note some bottoms emerged after similar crosses, one model projects a bottom ~54 days later.5
  • Study: prefunded decoy Ethereum wallets kept simulated ETH loss under $50. Researchers found many routers leak plaintext credentials, enabling theft risk to ETH during normal operation.6
  • Ondo Finance asked the SEC to let it use Ethereum to record and manage tokenized equity entitlements; tokens would track off-chain stocks/ETFs held via the Depository Trust Company.7
  • Analyst says recent Ethereum (ETHUSD) strength is likely temporary and expects ETH and related treasury holdings to resume declines after a brief pause; no price targets or timelines given.8

data.title: Nikkei May Rise After Overnight Gains on Wall Street — Market Talk
data.description: Japanese stocks may rise following overnight gains on Wall Street. Nikkei futures are up 2.0% at 57665 on the SGX. Investors are focusing on developments in the Middle East after U.S. military forces began blockading maritime traffic attempting to enter and leave Iranian ports. Crude oil prices are also being closely watched. The dollar is at 159.24 yen, compared with Y159.67 as of Monday's Tokyo stock market close. The Nikkei Stock Average fell 0.7% to 56502.77 on Monday. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com)

data.title: Antofagasta flags rising fuel costs, sees steady output as upgrades advance
data.description:
Key points:
  • Rising fuel and input costs are Antofagasta's main concern
  • Upgrades at Centinela and Los Pelambres mines coordinated to avoid operational disruptions
  • Few attractive M&A opportunities in copper sector, assets are rarely available or fully priced

By Fabian Cambero

Rising fuel prices remain Chilean miner Antofagasta Minerals' main cost concern, even as the company keeps operations on track and pushes ahead with upgrades at its Centinela and Los Pelambres mines, Chief Executive Ivan Arriagada told Reuters.

The miner is coordinating tie-ins between the modernization work and current operations at both mines to avoid disruptions, but has a schedule in place that should prevent setbacks, Arriagada said in an interview on Monday at his office in Santiago.

"In that sense, we are operating our plants in line with what we had planned," he said, reaffirming Antofagasta's 2026 production guidance of 650,000 to 700,000 metric tons of copper.

Arriagada said higher oil prices, and the resulting increase in fuel costs, are the company's biggest worry on the cost front.

"We have also seen increases in the price of some inputs: sulfuric acid, explosives, and to some extent, energy as well," he said. "That is happening in a context where the price of copper, while it has fallen, has generally remained at a favorable level."

He said the company has long-term contracts for sulfuric acid, which it uses in part of its operations, but is watching the market closely as delivered prices in Chile have risen.

Chile is a net importer of sulfuric acid for the copper industry, and global market conditions have altered trade flows, he said.

"If this worsens and supply shortages increase, it could affect us," Arriagada said.

Asked whether Antofagasta could cooperate with Glencore so copper from Argentina's El Pachon project could be exported through Chile using infrastructure tied to Los Pelambres, Arriagada said the upgrades at its flagship mine are intended for its own long-term development, though the company would assess any market opportunity on its merits.

"If there are synergy opportunities with a nearby deposit, we will have to evaluate them on their merits," he said, adding there was nothing specific to comment on now.

Arriagada also said he expects merger and acquisition attempts in the mining sector to continue, though attractive opportunities remain scarce.

"Most mining producers want to increase their exposure to copper. That means they are looking for opportunities," he said. "However, what we know is that copper is a market where deposits and opportunities in general are traded very infrequently."

As a result, few assets are available for sale and those that do come to market tend to be "fully priced," he said.

On the U.S. Twin Metals project, where Antofagasta is seeking to recover licenses, Arriagada said he hoped there could be progress given the importance Washington has placed on critical minerals.

He said Twin Metals has geological value, is located in an area with past and current iron mining activity, and could supply production to the local U.S. market.

data.title: AAL Stock Advances After-Hours Following Report Of UAL CEO Combining His Past And Present Companies
data.description:
Key points:
  • It is unclear if a process is underway to explore a deal, the report noted.
  • Kirby was previously President at American Airlines, before he switched over to United.
  • A combination between American Airlines and United Airlines, therefore, would create the largest airline on the planet and might pose antitrust concerns, Bloomberg noted.

Shares of American Airlines (AAL) jumped 5% on Monday following a media report that United Airlines (UAL) CEO Scott Kirby has floated a possible combination with the rival company.

According to a report from Bloomberg, citing people familiar with the matter, Kirby pitched the idea to senior government officials. It is unclear if a process is underway to explore a deal, the report noted. Kirby was previously President at American Airlines, before he switched over to United.

Currently, four major carriers — American Airlines (AAL), Delta Air Lines (DAL), United Airlines (UAL), and Southwest Airlines (LUV) — control the majority of the U.S. domestic passenger market. A combination between American Airlines and United Airlines would create the largest airline on the planet and might pose antitrust concerns, Bloomberg noted.

“I listened to most of our competitors at the J.P. Morgan conference this week and many said some version of ‘hope is our strategy.’ It's possible they're right and that the war ends quickly. But if it doesn't, this will be our opportunity down the road to buy assets, absorb network changes, etc.,” Kirby said last month in a memo to employees in light of jet fuel prices rising against the backdrop of the U.S.’s ongoing war with Iran and highlighting his interest in purchase opportunities.

Consolidation In Airlines

Earlier this month, U.S. transportation secretary Sean Duffy said in an interview with CNBC that there is room for consolidation in the industry. Trump loves to see big deals happen, Duffy said.

Duffy, however, reportedly added that any potential deal in the industry would face scrutiny on impact on ticket prices and competition. He also reportedly warned that companies will have to peel off some of their assets if there is a merger involving two major airlines in order to avoid a consolidation of market share.

Other Merger Talks

Last month, Semafor reported that JetBlue (JBLU) is considering selling itself to a rival airline and has tapped advisers to assess the viability of the plan. The company reportedly considered scenarios of how a deal with United Airlines, Alaska Airlines, or Southwest Airlines might fare in Washington.

JetBlue’s deal to acquire Spirit Airlines was blocked on antitrust grounds in 2024. U.S. airline mergers have to be reviewed and approved by the Transportation Department and the Department of Justice.

How Did Retail Traders React?

On Stocktwits, retail sentiment around AAL stock stayed within the ‘bearish’ territory over the past 24 hours, while message volume remained at ‘normal’ levels.

A Stocktwits user expressed pessimism about the airline sector in light of higher jet fuel prices and inflationary pressure.

https://www.stocktwits.com/traderheadnyc/message/650152295

Another cheered the AAL’s after-hours rally.

https://www.stocktwits.com/Debtfree2024/message/650174427

AAL stock has gained 17% over the past 12 months.

data.title: Brazil's Petrobras to resume construction on fertilizer unit in Mato Grosso do Sul state
data.description:
Brazilian state-run firm Petrobras approved on Monday the resumption of construction work at its UFN III nitrogen fertilizer plant in Mato Grosso do Sul state, the company said in a securities filing.

  • Petrobras will invest around $1 billion to complete the unit, with commercial operations expected to start in 2029, it said.

  • Brazilian President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva has pushed for the state-run firm to resume fertilizer production.

  • The shift also comes as Brazil aims to reduce supply risks amid the war in the Middle East, which threatens deliveries from a key source of the country's fertilizer imports.

  • Petrobras' Bahia and Sergipe fertilizer units also resumed operations in recent months.

data.title: If you've ever been tempted to 'sell in May and go away' - now is the time
data.description:
By Mark Hulbert

The stock market's 'Halloween Indicator' is statistically significant during just one year of the presidential cycle

Getting out of stocks until October could protect your portfolio from a mediocre market - or worse.

An elevated level of uncertainty prevails before the midterms. The resolution of this uncertainty boosts stocks after the election.

The coming year is the only one of the next four where stock investors should seriously consider following the old Wall Street adage: "Sell in May and go away."

I'm referring to the alleged seasonal pattern according to which stocks perform poorly between May Day and Halloween (the "summer" months) and better between Halloween and the subsequent May Day (the so-called winter months). This six-months-on, six-months-off pattern is also called the "Halloween indicator."

What few realize is that this pattern traces to just one of the four years of the U.S. presidential term - and it includes the next 12 months.

Notice in the chart above that the stock market on average performs far better over the six months after the November midterms than in the six months before - by 9.3 percentage points on average, which statistically is an extremely significant difference.

Across the other three years of the cycle, there is just a 1.3 percentage point difference, on average. This is not statistically significant.

Credit for discovering the Halloween indicator's dependence on the U.S. midterm election goes to Terry Marsh, an emeritus finance professor at the University of California, Berkeley, and Kam Fong Chan, a professor of finance at the University of Western Australia.

The professors traced this midterm pattern to the elevated level of uncertainty that prevails before the midterms and the resolution of that uncertainty after the midterms. Stocks tend to suffer because of that pre-midterm uncertainty and then soar as that uncertainty is resolved. (Interestingly, it doesn't seem to matter which party prevails in those midterms, since even an undesired outcome still provides the certainty with which corporations need to plan.)

Midterm elections are a greater source of uncertainty than presidential elections.

Many are surprised that the midterm elections are a greater source of uncertainty than presidential elections. But that's what the professors found upon analyzing seasonal patterns in the Economic Policy Uncertainty index. Their finding confirms what we already know about this year: A lot is riding on this year's midterms, regardless of political persuasions. Seemingly, everyone believes November's elections are a make-or-break moment for the U.S., with the prospect of a victory by the opposing side considered an existential threat.

Those who plan to mechanically follow this seasonal pattern over the next 12 months will be reducing their equity exposure at the end of April and restoring that exposure this coming Halloween. There are many variations on this approach, such as shifting to defensive sectors over the summer rather than going to cash.

The bottom line: If you've ever been tempted to sell in May, this is your best chance to sidestep a mediocre market - or worse.

Mark Hulbert is a regular contributor to MarketWatch. His Hulbert Ratings tracks investment newsletters that pay a flat fee to be audited. He can be reached at mark@hulbertratings.com

More: Keep calm and carry on buying the dip, says JPMorgan

Also read: Why 'Rule of 10' stocks like Nvidia and Meta are now poised for a comeback, according to Goldman Sachs

-Mark Hulbert

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.
